records.ety: [OF. contrevaloir; contre (L. contra) + valoir to avail, fr. L. valere to be strong, avail. See Vallant.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Countervailed (-v?ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Countervailing.]

defs: To act against with equal force, power, or effect; to thwart or overcome by such action; to furnish an equivalent to or for; to counterbalance; to compensate.
quotes: Upon balancing the account, the profit at last will hardly countervail the inconveniences that go allong with it.

defs: Power or value sufficient to obviate any effect; equal weight, strength, or value; equivalent; compensation; requital.
quotes: Surely, the present pleasure of a sinful act is a poor countervail for the bitterness of the review.
